reasoning: The index recently spiked to 6.122 on Friday, Dec 19. If the baseline value for Dec 21 (a Sunday) resolves to the Friday Dec 19 value (as is customary for non-publishing weekends), the threshold to exceed is a local maximum. Recent data shows a mean of ~6.04, with severe mean reversion, indicating a low probability of staying above 6.122 in the short term. The statistical linear model estimates a 26% probability for Dec 28 to exceed this figure. For later dates (Jan 20 and Mar 21), macroeconomic outlooks suggest mortgage rates will remain relatively flat or "tick down slightly" in early 2026, anchoring around the high-5s to low-6s. Still, as time horizon expands, volatility and drift increase uncertainty, pulling the probability closer to 0.5. I estimate 26% for Dec 28 (mean reversion strong), 36% for Jan 20, and 45% for Mar 21 (widening variance limits the effect of the local maximum start point).
